notes.photo_signal: Photos 1–2 are real home shots of a pink Minnie Mouse toy kitchen with doors open; many interior trays and accessories missing. Photo 3 looks like a retail or Amazon-style image with full accessories and toy food, unlike what is actually for sale.
notes.why_this_grade: Comparing the promo or retail image to the actual listing photos shows far too many missing accessories — not just a tray or two, enough to make the toy much less fun. Best-offer-only with no price adds hassle; easier to find another toy. Avoid the trip.
notes.pm_note: Seller language is slightly rude but also no-BS; neutral on tone. Dimension details in multiple forms are helpful. Promote to UI demo after eval pass — see build notes backlog.

notes.photo_signal: Three real home photos of a red interactive farm barn with silo; animals visible across angles match listed colors and types. Lights not shown on in photos; completeness of all pieces unclear across shots.
notes.why_this_grade: Leaning good — strong text-photo match and thoughtful listing effort — but not enough to drive yet: no price, unknown usage length, missing components unconfirmed, and interactive or light features not demonstrated in photos.
notes.pm_note: Do not grade on fun or value for money without price. Specific color details may be LLM-assisted seller text; still a positive listing-quality signal.

notes.photo_signal: Photo 1: real home shot of Robud wooden workbench beside bookshelves; looks like-new. Photo 2: Amazon product page screenshot showing $66.99 list price, 4.6 stars, and full product title.
notes.why_this_grade: Text-photo match is strong; seller smartly pairs a believable real photo with the original Amazon listing for price and specs. Brief text is offset by retail screenshot — straightforward lister, condition matches visuals. Trip-worthy with standard confirmation questions.
notes.pm_note: Brief seller text plus retail screenshot is a positive pattern — distinct from sparse listing with no price or product context.

notes.photo_signal: Real home photo of a pink and purple toy shopping cart on carpet. Teal plastic base has a large white stress mark and vertical crack between the wheels — looks snapped or structurally compromised.
notes.why_this_grade: Seller says fair and clean, but the photo shows obvious bottom/base damage that would make the cart unstable. Not worth a trip; at most ask for address or whether they'd give it away free.
notes.pm_note: Low-effort text plus visible damage is an Avoid pattern — photo contradicts the condition claim.

notes.photo_signal: Real home photo of a used Rolimat wooden activity set with xylophone, shape blocks, hammer, and balls on grey fabric. Xylophone mallet heads show heavy chipped blue paint; wood surfaces are scuffed. Red ball hole on top is empty, matching the missing-ball note.
notes.why_this_grade: Seller is honest about the missing red ball, but the set looks heavily worn with chipped paint on mallet heads and scuffed wood. For young kids who mouth toys, worn wooden paint is a safety concern — not worth the trip versus finding a better-condition set.
notes.pm_note: Worn paint on mouthing toys is a parent safety heuristic — model should weigh visible chipping even when seller is honest about the missing ball.

notes.photo_signal: Real home photo of a Dinosaur Paradise motorized track playset on wood-look flooring with two small dinosaur figures. Toy looks clean and intact with escalator track and tower signage visible. No power cord, battery compartment, or music controls visible from this angle.
notes.why_this_grade: Leaning good — the toy looks neat and the photo supports the like-new claim — but interactive features like music modes and the motorized lift cannot be verified from one photo. A parent needs power-source and working-order answers before driving.
notes.pm_note: Interactive toys with music or motor claims default to not_sure when power and working state are unstated and not visible in photos.

description: Condition: Used - Good. Grows with Kids: The removable floorboard allows the ride-on to transition between parent-controlled and kid-powered modes. Parent-Friendly Design: Features a comfortable rooftop handle for easy navigation by parents. Kid-Controlled Fun: Older kids can remove the floorboard and use their feet to roll around. Interactive Play: Includes a steering wheel, key, horn, and cup holders to keep toddlers engaged. Convenient Storage: Equipped with a trunk for easy storage access. Indoor & Outdoor Use: Durable tires are designed for both indoor and outdoor play. Easy Maneuverability: Front wheels spin 360 degrees for total control and easy maneuvering. Age & Weight Limit: Suitable for ages 18 months to 5 years, with a maximum weight limit of 50lbs.

notes.photo_signal: Real home photo on grey shag carpet of a plastic hedgehog peg-board toy with removable colored quills scattered beside it. Orange, green, purple, and red pegs visible — three of each color for twelve total matching twelve holes. Toy looks clean with no obvious damage.
notes.why_this_grade: $3 for a simple complete-looking peg toy is excellent value. Seller Mah San shows 4.5/5 stars, highly rated badge, and long Facebook tenure — credible listing. Photo supports used-good condition and completeness; trip-worthy with quick peg confirmation.
notes.pm_note: (9) in FB paste is review count not stars; star rating 4.5/5 provided as text in paste. Location San Jose parsed but must not affect grade.
